title: asperula graveolens subsp. leiograveolens
intro: Asperula graveolens subsp. leiograveolens, also known as Woodruff, is a perennial plant species within the Rubiaceae family, characterized by its white flowers and sweet, pungent fragrance. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic effects, which can help to soothe digestive issues and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Woodruff include its ability to reduce fever, relieve headaches, and act as a diuretic, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Woodruff can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
